skill.name: bibliography
skill.description: Classifies and organizes literature by theme, method, and conclusion; use when you need to batch-read a folder of PDF/MD/DOCX/TXT files and output a structured CSV for literature reviews and annotation management.
skill.summary: Batch-read and classify research papers by theme, method, and conclusion into structured CSV output.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 86 static analysis findings are false positives. The skill is a prompt-based Markdown instruction file for Claude and Codex, not executable code. Backtick patterns are Markdown inline code formatting for file extensions and path references. Cryptographic algorithm flags are keyword substring matches in JSON evaluation metadata and Markdown text. PowerShell flags are prohibition instructions telling the AI not to use PowerShell. System reconnaissance flags are legitimate file I/O descriptions for a literature bibliography tool. The only hardcoded URL is a GitHub attribution link to the skill source repository. No prompt injection or malicious intent detected.

low_findings.title: Markdown backtick formatting flagged as shell execution
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Static analyzer flagged over 70 instances of backtick-enclosed text as Ruby/shell backtick execution. All flagged instances are Markdown inline code formatting for file extensions (.pdf, .md, .docx, .txt), file paths (./outputs/), encoding names (GB18030, GBK), and placeholder values (Not recognized, Unidentified). This skill contains no executable shell code.

low_findings.title: Text keywords falsely flagged as cryptographic algorithm usage
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Static analyzer flagged 5 locations for 'Weak cryptographic algorithm'. None of these locations contain cryptographic code. bibliography_audit_result_v1.json:62 contains JSON evaluation metadata. references/guide.md:1 is the section title. SKILL.md:3 is the YAML description field. SKILL.md:133 discusses experimental methods extraction. SKILL.md:151 is the reference footer. The scanner likely matched the substring 'MD' (from Markdown file extension .md) as a cryptographic algorithm reference.

low_findings.title: PowerShell prohibition text flagged as PowerShell invocation
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Two lines instruct the AI to avoid PowerShell for CSV operations. references/guide.md:45 states 'It is strictly forbidden to use PowerShell to directly write or manipulate CSV/Markdown.' SKILL.md:147 states 'Do not use PowerShell to directly write/manipulate CSV/Markdown to avoid encoding/newline issues.' These are safety prohibitions, not invocations.

low_findings.title: File I/O descriptions falsely flagged as system reconnaissance
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Static analyzer flagged 6 locations as 'System reconnaissance.' These are legitimate file processing descriptions for a literature bibliography tool: UTF-8 encoding specifications, placeholder values for missing fields, file cleanup instructions, and output directory management. These are standard file I/O operations documented for a bibliography management workflow.

low_findings.title: Source repository URL flagged as network concern
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: SKILL.md:7 contains a Markdown-formatted hyperlink to the GitHub source repository (github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills). This is a standard attribution link and is not an exfiltration endpoint. The skill performs no network requests during operation.

content.user_title: Organize academic literature into structured summaries and CSV
content.value_statement: Researchers waste hours manually summarizing papers and organizing references. This skill batch-processes folders of PDF, DOCX, MD, and TXT files into structured CSV output with theme, method, and conclusion fields for immediate use in analysis tools.
actual_capabilities: Batch process PDF, DOCX, MD, and TXT files from an input directory for literature review
Extract title, abstract, keywords, experimental methods, and key conclusions from each document
Generate a human-readable summary Markdown file with structured per-document entries
Produce a machine-friendly CSV file following the field order and headers from a template
Handle PDF conversion using pdf-extract tool with automatic path detection and user fallback
Fill missing extraction fields with standardized placeholder values to prevent empty cells
limitations: Does not perform original research or fact-check claims made in papers
Quality of extraction depends on the text layer quality of PDF documents
Cannot process image-only PDFs or scanned documents without external OCR tools
Requires the pdf-extract external tool for PDF to Markdown conversion

use_cases.title: Graduate thesis literature review
use_cases.description: A graduate student collects 30 papers in PDF and DOCX format for their thesis literature review chapter. The skill reads all files, generates consistent summaries, and produces a CSV that can be sorted and filtered by theme and method.

use_cases.title: Research lab paper curation
use_cases.description: A lab manager maintains a shared folder of weekly journal club readings. The skill batch-processes new additions and updates a master CSV so all lab members can search and filter by topic, method, or conclusion.

use_cases.title: Systematic review screening
use_cases.description: A clinical researcher screens abstracts from a batch of retrieved studies. The skill extracts keywords and methods into a structured format, enabling rapid filtering and categorization during the screening phase.

prompt_templates.title: Basic folder summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Read all literature files in the inputs folder and generate a summary Markdown file with title, keywords, methods, and conclusions for each document. Save the output to the outputs folder.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick overview of a small set of papers

prompt_templates.title: Structured CSV generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Process the literature folder and generate a CSV with columns for filename, file type, keywords, experimental methods, conclusion points, abstract, and comments. Follow the field order and headers from the assets/bibliography_template.csv template.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating a machine-readable bibliography for data analysis

prompt_templates.title: Full pipeline with PDF conversion
prompt_templates.prompt: Batch-read the mixed-format papers in the inputs directory. First convert any PDF files to Markdown using pdf-extract. Then generate a summary Markdown file and finally a CSV following the template. Output both files to the outputs directory.
prompt_templates.scenario: Handling a mixed-format folder with PDF documents

prompt_templates.title: Multi-language abstract processing
prompt_templates.prompt: Process the literature folder with papers in both English and Chinese. For English papers, translate abstracts and conclusions to Chinese. Output the summary Markdown and CSV with consistent Chinese field headers and descriptions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Bilingual literature review with Chinese output format

output_examples.input: A folder containing three research papers in PDF and DOCX format about machine learning in healthcare
output_examples.output: A summary Markdown file is created with structured entries for each paper. Each entry includes the title, a 3-5 sentence summary, pipe-separated keywords, experimental methods list, one-sentence key conclusion, and one-sentence commentary. A matching CSV file is also generated with the same data in tabular format.

output_examples.input: A single DOCX file containing a research paper abstract and methods section
output_examples.output: A summary Markdown entry with the extracted title, abstract text, 3 generated keywords with the note '(generated based on abstract)', the methods mentioned in the paper, and a single-sentence conclusion. The CSV row fills any missing fields with 'Not recognized'.

best_practices: Place all input files in a single folder before running the skill for consistent batch processing.
Keep PDF files with intact text layers for best extraction quality and accuracy.
Review the summary Markdown output before using the generated CSV for downstream analysis.
anti_patterns: Do not mix input reading with other analysis tasks such as data visualization or statistical testing.
Do not modify the CSV template header order in assets/bibliography_template.csv as the skill relies on exact field positions.
Do not skip the summary Markdown generation step as it is the single source of truth for the final CSV output.

faq.question: What file formats does this skill support?
faq.answer: PDF, DOCX, MD, and TXT file formats are supported for input.

faq.question: How are missing fields handled in the output?
faq.answer: Any field that cannot be extracted uses the text Not recognized as a placeholder to avoid empty cells.

faq.question: Does this skill require external tools to function?
faq.answer: PDF files require the pdf-extract external tool for conversion to Markdown before text extraction.

faq.question: Can this skill process scanned PDF documents?
faq.answer: No. Scanned PDFs without an embedded text layer cannot be processed without additional OCR tools.

faq.question: What output files does the skill generate?
faq.answer: The skill creates exactly two output files: a summary Markdown file and a CSV file in the outputs folder.

faq.question: Is the generated CSV compatible with spreadsheet software?
faq.answer: Yes. The CSV uses UTF-8 encoding and is compatible with Excel, R, Python, and similar data analysis tools.
